message.content:
Read the file at "C:\Users\lny03\Desktop\新建文件夹 (2)\hse1.apifox.json" and find all API endpoints related to IncidentReport. For each endpoint, extract:
1. The HTTP method and path
2. The full request body schema (all fields, types, nested objects)
3. The response body schema

Focus especially on:
- saveDetailIncidentReport (or similar save/update endpoint)
- incidentReportDetail (get detail endpoint)
- Any endpoint related to external parties, employees, properties, issues

Output the complete field names and types in a structured format. This is for aligning frontend form fields with backend API.

Search thoroughly through the entire file since it may be large.

attachment.content:
- brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Perfect! I've found the IncidentReport API specification file. Let me extract all the relevant endpoints and their schemas.

## INCIDENT REPORT API ENDPOINTS

Based on the Apifox JSON file, here are all the IncidentReport-related endpoints with complete schema details:

### 1. **saveDetail** - Save/Update Incident Report Details
**Endpoint:** 
- HTTP Method: `POST`
- Path: `/api/incident/report/save/detail`

**Request Body Schema: `IncidentReportDetailSaveRequest`**
```
{
  incidentReportId: integer (int64) - Optional, existing report ID for edit; null for new
  incidentDate: string (DATETIME) - Accepts dd/MM/yyyy or dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm format
  locationSystem: string
  locationSystemAr: string
  incidentDetails: string (English)
  incidentDetailsAr: string (Arabic)
  otherImpactAr: string - Other Impact description (Arabic)
  actionsTakenAr: string - Immediate Actions Taken by The Team (Arabic)
  otherImpact: string - Other Impact description (English)
  actionsTaken: string - Immediate Actions Taken by The Team (English)
  convertedAccident: integer - Is incident converted to accident? (0=No, 1=Yes)
  externalPartyInvolved: integer - (0=No, 1=Yes)
  employeeImpacted: integer - (0=No, 1=Yes)
  propertyImpacted: integer - (0=No, 1=Yes)
  otherImpacted: integer - (0=No, 1=Yes)
  
  // Section rows
  externalParties: array[IncidentReportExternalPartySaveRequest]
  issues: array[IncidentReportIssueSaveRequest]
  incidentReportDetailEmployeeSaveRequests: array[IncidentReportDetailEmployeeSaveRequest]
  incidentReportDetailPropertySaveRequests: array[IncidentReportDetailPropertySaveRequest]
  attachmentList: array[AttachmentDTO]
}
```

**Response Body Schema: `ApiResultLong`**
```
{
  code: string - Return status code
  msg: string - Return message
  data: integer (int64) - The saved incident report ID
}
```

---

### 2. **External Parties Sub-Schema** - `IncidentReportExternalPartySaveRequest`
Used in the `externalParties` array field:
```
{
  id: integer (int64) - Optional; existing row ID for edit, null for new row
  partyType: integer - 1=Police, 2=Ambulance, 3=Fire Department, 4=Others
  responseDetailsEn: string (English) - Response details
  responseDetailsAr: string (Arabic) - Response details
  attachmentList: array[AttachmentDTO] - Related attachments
}
```

---

### 3. **Issues Sub-Schema** - `IncidentReportIssueSaveRequest`
Used in the `issues` array field:
```
{
  id: integer (int64) - Issue identifier
  notesRemarksEn: string (English)
  notesRemarksAr: string (Arabic)
  correctiveActionEn: string (English) - Corrective action
  correctiveActionAr: string (Arabic) - Corrective action
  responsibleOrgId: integer (int64) - Organization ID responsible for closure
  targetClosureDate: string (DATE format) - Date target for closure
  attachmentList: array[AttachmentDTO] - Related attachments
}
```

---

### 4. **Employees Sub-Schema** - `IncidentReportDetailEmployeeSaveRequest`
Used in the `incidentReportDetailEmployeeSaveRequests` array field:
```
{
  userId: integer (int64) - Employee user ID
  title: string - Employee title/position
  natureOfInjury: string (English) - Type of injury suffered
  natureOfInjuryAr: string (Arabic) - Type of injury suffered
}
```

---

### 5. **Properties Sub-Schema** - `IncidentReportDetailPropertySaveRequest`
Used in the `incidentReportDetailPropertySaveRequests` array field:
```
{
  propertyImpactedId: integer (int64) - Property identifier
  propertyName: string (English) - Name of property
  propertyNameAr: string (Arabic) - Name of property
  natureOfDamage: string (English) - Type/description of damage
  natureOfDamageAr: string (Arabic) - Type/description of damage
}
```

---

### 6. **Attachment Sub-Schema** - `AttachmentDTO`
Used in multiple attachment arrays across the main request and sub-schemas:
```
{
  id: integer (int64) - Attachment ID
  attachmentId: integer (int64)
  uniqueId: integer (int64) - External unique ID
  url: string - Attachment URL/path
  fileName: string - Attachment file name
  fileType: string - File type (e.g., "pdf", "jpg")
  size: integer (int64) - File size in bytes
  sort: integer - Sort order
  type: integer - Attachment business type
  bytes: array[integer] - Raw file bytes
  contentType: string - MIME content type (e.g., "application/pdf")
  absUrl: string - Absolute URL path
}
```

---

## SUMMARY FOR FRONTEND FORM FIELDS

**Main Form Sections:**
1. **Basic Info:** incidentReportId, incidentDate, locationSystem, locationSystemAr
2. **Incident Description:** incidentDetails, incidentDetailsAr, actionsTaken, actionsTakenAr
3. **Impact Flags:** externalPartyInvolved, employeeImpacted, propertyImpacted, otherImpacted, otherImpact, convertedAccident
4. **Dynamic Sections:** External Parties, Employees, Properties, Issues
5. **Attachments:** attachmentList (can attach to main report and sub-sections)

All text fields support bilingual (English/Arabic) content. All boolean flags are stored as integers (0/1). All date fields use specific formats (DATE or DATETIME).
